'''Child''' is one of the four [[grammatical genders]] in [[Aelaki]]. It corresponds to the pre-reproductive life stage of the speaker's species, which are sequential hermaphrodites progressing from child to [[Female|female]] to [[Male|male]].

== Overview ==

The child gender is used for animate nouns referring to beings in their pre-reproductive stage. For invertebrates and artefacts that lack a clear biological life stage, gender may be assigned by perceived age or sound symbolism; young or small entities tend to receive child gender.

== Vowel pattern ==

{| class="wikitable"
|-
! Number !! Vowel
|-
| Singular || '''u'''
|-
| Collective || '''i'''
|}

== Ki syllable table ==

The following table shows the [[Ki syllable|ki syllables]] for child gender across all four persons, in singular, collective, and [[Inanimate|inanimate]]-marked forms:

{| class="wikitable"
|-
! Person !! Singular !! Reduplication !! Collective !! Inanimate-marked
|-
| 1st person || thu || thuthu || thi || thuf
|-
| 2nd person || ju || juju || ji || juf
|-
| 3rd person || shu || shushu || shi || shuf
|-
| 4th person || ku || kuku || ki || kuf
|}

== Relationship to other genders ==

As [[Aelaki]] speakers mature, they transition biologically from child to [[Female|female]] to [[Male|male]]. Nouns tracking a living being normally shift gender to match the being's current life stage. The [[Inanimate|inanimate]] gender stands apart, reserved for non-living and mass nouns.

== See also ==

* [[Female]]
* [[Male]]
* [[Inanimate]]

[[Category:grammatical genders]]

[[Category:git synced pages]]
